narrative:
William Saliba is a Centre-Back representing Cameroon at the World Cup. He holds an overall rating of 7.1/10, placing him in the top 7% of centre-backs at this tournament. This rating reflects both his recent club form and his current World Cup performances.

At this World Cup, Saliba demonstrates strong defensive positioning, evidenced by his 1.25 interceptions and 4.75 clearances per 90 minutes. However, his offensive contribution is minimal, with 0.013 expected goals and 0.016 expected assists per 90 minutes. He averages only 0.25 shots and 0.5 tackles per 90 minutes in the tournament.

Saliba is clearly a crucial defensive anchor for Cameroon, having played 360 minutes in the tournament. Scouts should continue to monitor his consistent defensive output, particularly his ability to make 1.25 interceptions and 4.75 clearances per 90 minutes. His role is to primarily nullify opposition attacks rather than initiate them.
